jokes.text: I had Grizz call him at 8:00 this morning and pretend it was 11:00. I printed up that fake rehearsal schedule for him, Saying we were starting at 9:00 instead of noon. Oh, and I set all his watches and clocks to say P.M. when it's really A.M.
jokes.setup: Simple problem of Tracy being late
jokes.punchline: Elaborate time manipulation scheme that backfired spectacularly
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing how overcompensating creates bigger problems. Classic 30 Rock absurdism with logical internal consistency.

jokes.text: I took my son to his cello recital this morning at what turned out to be midnight yesterday!
jokes.setup: Tracy's anger at the time confusion
jokes.punchline: Specific absurd consequence of dragging child to midnight cello recital
jokes.explanation: Perfect specificity makes the absurd consequence feel real and funnier. Great character voice.

jokes.text: You treat me like one of those little pageant girls with the clip-on teeth.
jokes.setup: Tracy complaining about being treated like a child
jokes.punchline: Weirdly specific and disturbing comparison to beauty pageant contestants
jokes.explanation: Disturbing and specific imagery that's perfectly Tracy. The clip-on teeth detail elevates it.

jokes.text: Irregardless!
jokes.setup: Tracy making an impassioned argument
jokes.punchline: Uses fake word 'irregardless' undermining his credibility
jokes.explanation: Simple malapropism but perfectly timed and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: You know what? Race card. No, don't accept it!
jokes.setup: Tracy losing argument and resorting to race accusations
jokes.punchline: Treats 'race card' as literal card that can be rejected
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta joke literalizing a common metaphor. Visual component of card exchange elevates it.

jokes.text: There's a restaurant hostess in midtown I'd like to see cry.
jokes.setup: Jonathan offering to take Jack's mother out
jokes.punchline: Reveals Jonathan's cruel motivation for restaurant choice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonathan character moment - petty cruelty delivered matter-of-factly.

jokes.text: Including more than one Unitarian!
jokes.setup: Jack listing his father's terrible behavior with women
jokes.punchline: Treating Unitarianism as scandalous as adultery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack snobbery - treating religious denomination as moral failing. Shows his warped priorities.

jokes.text: And I once made love to Kathy Hilton.
jokes.setup: Jack saying father abandonment is his biggest regret
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of regrettable sexual encounter with Paris Hilton's mother
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between emotional vulnerability and casual sexual confession. Pure Jack.

jokes.text: What is this, the Italian parliament?
jokes.setup: Liz offering Jack a hug for comfort
jokes.punchline: Jack's bizarre reference to Italian parliament being touchy-feely
jokes.explanation: Oddly specific cultural reference that's perfectly Jack's pretentious deflection style.

jokes.text: Evidently, the concierge at the plaza 'has a beard,' and she'd rather not get raped.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Colleen changed hotels
jokes.punchline: Colleen's paranoid leap from facial hair to sexual assault
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing Colleen's paranoid worldview. Uncomfortable but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Tracy hasn't come back yet, But he did sent a gibbon to rehearse in his place.
jokes.setup: Tracy's absence from rehearsal
jokes.punchline: Literal monkey replacement for human actor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of Tracy's diva behavior. Sets up ongoing monkey subplot beautifully.

jokes.text: [laughs] he's pretty good.
jokes.setup: Gibbon replacing Tracy at rehearsal
jokes.punchline: Casual assessment that monkey is competent actor
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery. The casual acceptance makes the absurdity funnier.

jokes.text: Dear racist Liz Lemon... this is how you treat me, like a white-whiskered gibbon put on this earth to do nothing but dance around for your amusement And reduce the insect population of Malaysia.
jokes.setup: Tracy's formal complaint about treatment
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely specific gibbon comparison with Malaysian insect detail
jokes.explanation: Fantastic specificity and escalation. The Malaysian insect detail is perfect Tracy absurdity.

jokes.text: Why? The gibbon is on time, he knows his blocking, And he doesn't try to bite the dancers!
jokes.setup: Liz wanting Tracy back from his protest
jokes.punchline: Pete making case that gibbon is more professional than Tracy
jokes.explanation: Great callback that escalates the absurdity while making a logical point about Tracy's behavior.

jokes.text: Time jump!
jokes.setup: Tracy walking down stairs slowly
jokes.punchline: Liz breaking the fourth wall to skip boring parts
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke that acknowledges TV pacing conventions while serving the story efficiently.

jokes.text: Don't patronize me with your Celtic slang, Liz Lemon.
jokes.setup: Liz using word 'shenanigans'
jokes.punchline: Tracy misidentifying common word as ethnic slur
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy paranoia and miseducation. 'Shenanigans' as Celtic slang is wonderfully wrong.

jokes.text: What do you care? You voted for Nader!
jokes.setup: Discussion about post-racial America
jokes.punchline: Tracy accurately calling out Liz's liberal guilt through voting history
jokes.explanation: Sharp character insight - Tracy reading Liz perfectly through political choices.

jokes.text: Then tomorrow, I'm sending a regular town car for you Instead of one of those duck tour boats.
jokes.setup: Tracy wanting equal treatment
jokes.punchline: Reveals Tracy's transportation is absurdly elaborate duck boat
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation revealing how ridiculous Tracy's normal treatment is. Duck boat is inspired.

jokes.text: And you're no longer allowed to point at women In the cafeteria and yell, 'I wanna get that pregnant!'
jokes.setup: Liz listing Tracy's privileges being revoked
jokes.punchline: Reveals Tracy's disturbing cafeteria behavior
jokes.explanation: Shockingly specific and inappropriate behavior that's perfectly Tracy. Instantly quotable.

jokes.text: Fine, I'll bring my lunch from home.
jokes.setup: Tracy being told he can't sexually harass cafeteria workers
jokes.punchline: Treats it as minor inconvenience, missing the point entirely
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Tracy's complete disconnect from normal social behavior.

jokes.text: [Extended mutual laughter escalation]
jokes.setup: Tracy promising to have the last laugh
jokes.punchline: Literal laugh-off that escalates absurdly
jokes.explanation: Visual gag that works better on screen. Good escalation but loses impact in text.

jokes.text: You humiliate Tracy in front of everybody like that, You give him two choices... one, he won't show up tomorrow, And you'll look like a fool in front of your crew. Or two, he will show up... and he'll find an even worse way to act out.
jokes.setup: Explaining consequences of embarrassing Tracy publicly
jokes.punchline: Second option is somehow worse than not showing up
jokes.explanation: Good setup for what's coming. Dotcom's wisdom about Tracy's psychology.

jokes.text: Paul, this is Jack, my son. Jack, this is Paul Brett.
jokes.setup: Colleen introducing her boyfriend to Jack
jokes.punchline: Awkward family dynamic with age-inappropriate relationship
jokes.explanation: Situational comedy from uncomfortable family meeting. Sets up tension well.

jokes.text: Paul lives in Florida. He paints landscapes and he makes his own soda.
jokes.setup: Colleen describing her boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Hilariously unimpressive accomplishments presented proudly
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation - homemade soda as a selling point. Shows Colleen's low standards.

jokes.text: Great! I'll call the restaurant. See if they can seat a third wheel.
jokes.setup: Being invited to join mother's date
jokes.punchline: Jack's bitter self-awareness of his position
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating humor showing Jack's discomfort with the situation.

jokes.text: Now let's get outta here Before we realize we've forgotten about something.
jokes.setup: End of workday
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging they're definitely forgetting something important
jokes.explanation: Meta awareness of sitcom structure. Sets up the monkey subplot being forgotten.

jokes.text: That wasn't me screaming in the bathroom.
jokes.setup: Jenna entering awkwardly
jokes.punchline: Immediate denial of embarrassing bathroom incident
jokes.explanation: Classic Jenna oversharing. The specific denial makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Well, don't worry. Once it tries to mate with a child, I'm sure animal control will just shoot it.
jokes.setup: Concern about releasing the gibbon
jokes.punchline: Casually predicting disturbing outcome with bureaucratic certainty
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark Kenneth moment. The matter-of-fact delivery of horrible prediction is perfect.

jokes.text: Oh, he loves me! Somebody loves me. I'm gonna name him little Jenna And let him live in my dressing room.
jokes.setup: Jenna bonding with the gibbon
jokes.punchline: Desperate need for affection leading to inappropriate monkey adoption
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna narcissism and desperation. Naming monkey after herself is brilliant character detail.

jokes.text: When the waiter brought over the food, he said, 'abbondanza.'
jokes.setup: Jack listing suspicious things about Paul
jokes.punchline: Italian phrase treated as red flag
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Jack's paranoid analysis of innocent behavior.

jokes.text: Have you ever been to Florida? It's basically a criminal population. It's America's Australia.
jokes.setup: Jack's suspicion about Paul being from Florida
jokes.punchline: Comparing Florida to Australia's criminal origins
jokes.explanation: Fantastic analogy that's both historically clever and perfectly Jack's snobbery. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Let's...Not make this about you, okay?
jokes.setup: Jack's rant about Florida criminals
jokes.punchline: Liz's weary recognition of Jack's narcissism
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's familiarity with Jack's self-centeredness.

jokes.text: You know, this food area Is always the first place I go to look for you...
jokes.setup: Cerie finding Liz in catering
jokes.punchline: Casually noting Liz's predictable food-seeking behavior
jokes.explanation: Gentle ribbing about Liz's eating habits. Cerie's innocent observation style.

jokes.text: Professional is my middle name. No need. I've got it memorized.
jokes.setup: Tracy claiming to be professional
jokes.punchline: Immediately proves he's lying by claiming to have memorized his middle name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy logic - needs to memorize his own middle name. Self-defeating in classic Tracy fashion.

jokes.text: I'm going to do a Valentino cross, Camera right, then dump the laugh. So stay on your fours, guys! One, two, three, jump and pose!
jokes.setup: Tracy being overly professional
jokes.punchline: Spouting nonsensical director jargon mixed with real terms
jokes.explanation: Good mix of real and fake show business terminology. Shows Tracy's surface knowledge.

jokes.text: Not Indians. Not whatever this guy is.
jokes.setup: Tracy listing groups that shouldn't get preferential treatment
jokes.punchline: Casual racism toward unidentified crew member
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable but character-appropriate moment showing Tracy's obliviousness to his own prejudice.

jokes.text: I'm taking a photography class. Look at the way the sunlight hits that cobweb.
jokes.setup: Private investigator explaining why he's at the location
jokes.punchline: Artistic photography interest while on surveillance
jokes.explanation: Good character subversion - gruff PI with artistic sensibilities, delivered matter-of-factly.

jokes.text: For instance, I'm wearing a child's Halloween costume Under this.
jokes.setup: PI explaining everyone has secrets
jokes.punchline: Reveals his own bizarre secret about wearing children's clothing
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully weird and specific detail. Perfect example of 30 Rock's random character quirks.

jokes.text: His costume is hiding his erection.
jokes.setup: Jenna defending keeping the monkey as happy
jokes.punchline: Disturbing revelation about monkey's arousal state
jokes.explanation: Appropriately disturbing detail. Shows Jenna's obliviousness to inappropriate situation.

jokes.text: She bit off my nut sack That I kept tied around my belt to feed squirrels.
jokes.setup: Kenneth's pig story seeming to go in disturbing direction
jokes.punchline: Reveals 'nut sack' is literally a bag of nuts for squirrels
jokes.explanation: Masterful misdirection. Audience assumes the worst meaning, then gets innocent but still weird truth.

jokes.text: I realize I'm never getting married Because of my gland thing... But I wanna have a bachelor party.
jokes.setup: Lutz wanting a bachelor party
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging he'll never marry due to medical condition but still wanting celebration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lutz patheticness. Self-aware but still hopeful in a sad way.

jokes.text: It's like orange-flavored egg! It's in my mouth!
jokes.setup: Frank releasing held-back fart
jokes.punchline: Liz's disgustingly specific description of smell sensation
jokes.explanation: Gross but specific imagery. The orange-flavored detail makes it more vivid and disgusting.

jokes.text: Well, I pay you a little less, yes.
jokes.setup: Liz asking if Jack treats her differently as a woman
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of wage discrimination
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack directness about terrible behavior. Casual admission makes it funnier and more disturbing.

jokes.text: That's the quantum leap intro.
jokes.setup: Jack's speech about going back to put things right
jokes.punchline: Liz recognizing it as TV show premise
jokes.explanation: Nice meta moment showing Liz's TV knowledge. Quick reference that enhances character dynamic.

jokes.text: Who would be our current Nikita Khrushchev? Mm...Simon Cowell?
jokes.setup: Jack updating his old anti-father speech
jokes.punchline: Comparing reality TV judge to Soviet leader
jokes.explanation: Funny modern update attempt. Shows both characters' pop culture awareness vs historical knowledge.

jokes.text: Now you're standing on the very spot Where Gracie Allen took Jack Paar's virginity.
jokes.setup: NBC studio tour
jokes.punchline: Disturbing historical detail about beloved comedy stars
jokes.explanation: Dark historical rewriting of wholesome TV history. Good shock value with classic stars.

jokes.text: Little Jenna had a baby last night.
jokes.setup: Jenna announcing news about her pet monkey
jokes.punchline: Treating monkey reproduction like human family announcement
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of monkey subplot. Jenna's maternal projection onto primate.

jokes.text: Let's go see some naked daughters and moms!
jokes.setup: Going to strip club for bachelor party
jokes.punchline: Framing strippers as family members rather than sexual objects
jokes.explanation: Disturbing reframe that's somehow more uncomfortable than direct objectification.

jokes.text: I bet you can, you son of a bitch.
jokes.setup: Paul mentioning you can check out on hotel TV
jokes.punchline: Jack's paranoid interpretation of innocent comment
jokes.explanation: Shows Jack's antagonistic mindset. Everything Paul says sounds suspicious to him.

jokes.text: My credit card company called to confirm My purchase of a book entitled intercourse after hip surgery.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining how he knows his mother is back
jokes.punchline: Specific, embarrassing book purchase
jokes.explanation: Good character detail that's both specific and uncomfortable, typical of awkward mother-son dynamic.

jokes.text: That's right. I just called you a communist.
jokes.setup: Jack calling Paul 'Simon Cowell'
jokes.punchline: Jack's confused equation of reality TV judge with communism
jokes.explanation: Great callback showing Jack's confused attempt to update his speech. Political confusion is perfect.

jokes.text: It's Florida, Jack! It's like it never stopped being the '70s down there.
jokes.setup: Jack's shock about Colleen knowing Paul is married
jokes.punchline: Colleen's casual explanation of Florida culture
jokes.explanation: Great callback to Jack's earlier Florida criticism, now validated by his mother's casual acceptance of questionable morality.

jokes.text: It's Florida, Jack! It's like it never stopped being the '70s down there. And a guy like Paul, who can drive at night, You just don't say no to that!
jokes.setup: Colleen explaining her relationship with Paul
jokes.punchline: Standards so low that night vision is a selling point
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Colleen's pragmatic approach to aging. Night driving as romantic qualification is perfect.

jokes.text: That man left and came back so many times That I totally lost count! One night, he went out to move the car, And he was gone... From the spring of '57 until... One night he just showed up out of the blue! 'put your shoes on, Collie. We're goin' to see some like it hot.'
jokes.setup: Jack's traumatic memory of father leaving
jokes.punchline: Reveals father's departures were routine, not traumatic single event
jokes.explanation: Excellent character revelation that reframes Jack's entire backstory. The casual movie date detail is perfect.

jokes.text: You're nothing like him, you know. No way you ever could be.
jokes.setup: Colleen comforting Jack about not being like his father
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment that sounds more like an insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect Colleen - attempting comfort but making it sound like Jack lacks his father's appeal.

jokes.setup: Colleen calling their 6 PM dinner 'late'
jokes.punchline: Paul's confused correction about the early hour
jokes.explanation: Simple age-gap humor. Shows generational difference in dining schedules.

jokes.text: Say you like my body.
jokes.setup: Bachelor party at strip club
jokes.punchline: Uncomfortably direct demand from stripper
jokes.explanation: Appropriately uncomfortable for strip club setting, though not particularly original or clever.

jokes.text: [crying] I don't wanna be here! I don't like it here! Who's this guy?
jokes.setup: Lutz's bachelor party at strip club
jokes.punchline: Complete breakdown at his own party
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to Lutz's fake bachelor party concept - his fantasy becomes a nightmare he can't handle.

jokes.text: You said you loved me! Your foot fingers are so strong!
jokes.setup: Jenna's breakdown over losing monkey
jokes.punchline: Disturbing details about their inappropriate relationship
jokes.explanation: 'Foot fingers' is perfect weird detail. Shows how deeply Jenna projected human qualities onto monkey.

jokes.text: One of those strippers took of Lutz's shirt. That gland thing is no joke!
jokes.setup: Frank explaining why he can't take the strip club
jokes.punchline: Visual confirmation of Lutz's mysterious gland condition
jokes.explanation: Good callback that finally reveals the visual reality of Lutz's mysterious condition, making it funnier through Frank's traumatized reaction.

jokes.text: We're dressing monkeys up as people, And monkeys are playing with people as toys!
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining how the natural order is upset
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the literal monkey-human role reversal
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-observation that ties together the monkey subplot with the main theme about social order.

jokes.text: The gibbon did not attack Jenna. He was trying to mate with her face.
jokes.setup: Clarifying rumors about monkey incident
jokes.punchline: Correction makes situation worse rather than better
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth delivery making horrible situation sound routine. Great escalation of monkey subplot.

jokes.text: 1959. Boy, it would have been fun to write back then. You could get away with crazy plot twists Because audiences were so much less sophisticated.
jokes.setup: Liz commenting on old movie era
jokes.punchline: Ironic statement given the absurd plot twist coming
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta setup for the paternity revelation. Self-aware about its own plot mechanics.

jokes.text: This movie came out in 1959? How could my father have been gone From the spring of '57 until 1959 if I was conceived in '58... Jimmy Donaghy's not my father.
jokes.setup: Jack's sentimental memory of parents seeing movie together
jokes.punchline: Mathematical revelation that destroys his entire family history
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and payoff. Simple math destroys Jack's worldview. Great callback to Liz's comment about plot twists.

jokes.text: This movie came out in 1959? How could my father have been gone From the spring of '57 until 1959 if I was conceived in '58...
jokes.setup: Jack watching 'Some Like It Hot' and doing timeline math
jokes.punchline: Realizing the mathematical impossibility of his paternity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that uses earlier movie reference to create major plot revelation through simple math.

jokes.text: [gasps] Twist!
jokes.setup: Jack's paternity revelation
jokes.punchline: Liz treating real life like TV show plot development
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to her earlier comment about unsophisticated audiences. Great meta moment.
